_readme: APPROVED recipe: every knob pinned (a later generic-default change cannot restyle this look). starcomb resolution: CLI > this file > GENERIC.

notes.starless_denoise: off, not the generic vstpost: this is a high-SNR real-colour target, and the post-stretch VST (a self-flat-class denoise adaptation) over-neutralizes bright-object chroma sitting near the grain scale, flattening real colour into soft patches. off keeps the real chroma at gate PASS; the linear GraXpert denoise (gx) tints the sky.
notes.spcc: broadband RGB from a mono filter wheel: SPCC runs sensor-null on the composed stack (the generic default; a known sensor curve is an immaterial refinement here)
notes.solve: narrow 53-arcmin field at 0.68"/px (FOCALLEN 1150 mm): solve with a position hint + cached index scales -- solve_field.py --ra=270.6 --dec=-23.0 --radius-deg=10 --scales=4-9 (scales 1-3 are multi-GB index downloads; scale 4 carries the solution)
notes.ladders: ladders run (cull_pct, black_point, starless_denoise): generic values kept wherever no dataset-specific reason to move showed; starless_denoise off is the one exception (see its note)
notes.tool_migration: tool-only migration: the in-house numpy corings (chroma_core/lum_core) were removed from the chain, so this recipe no longer applies them — a declared delta. The final saturation, star MTF, black point and screen combine now drive siril (satu/mtf/pm). Look + baseline need re-judgment + rebaseline; this recipe was approved for its denoise/anchor choices, not the corings.

approved.note: aura reads high here only because the fixed-px shell annuli sit in the star-halo zone at this pixel scale (scale-awareness, BACKLOG); no visible ghost aura at 1:1 -- baseline recorded with --ack-aura-warn
